title: WEATHERIZATION AND ENERGY CONSERVATION - 211info
text: BEAVERTON MULTI-SERVICE CENTER WEATHERIZATION AND ENERGY CONSERVATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides weatherization services to help households lower their utility bills. Services include in-home visits by an educator/auditor who provides energy-saving tips, efficient lighting, and water-saving devices. Households may also qualify for comprehensive weatherization services that include an energy audit visit to determine which energy-saving measures can be installed. Private contractors then install the recommended cost-effective measures, which may include ceiling, floor, or wall insulation; EPDM roofs (a durable synthetic rubber material); duct sealing; water heaters; heating systems; exhaust fans; or energy-efficient window and refrigerator replacements. 17933 NW Evergreen Place Suite 315 Portland, OR 97229 Eligibility: Homeowners and renters earning no more than 200% of the Federal Poverty Level: -1-person household: $25,520/year or $2,126/month gross -2-person household: $34,480/year or $2,873/month gross -3-person household: $43,440/year or $3,620.00/month gross -4-person household: $52,400/year or $4,366/month gross -5-person household: $61,360/year or $5,113/month gross Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 906-6550 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 906-6550 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for screening and application or download an application from the website. Wait times before first visit vary by heating source, but the initial wait could be 4-6 weeks. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Proof of income LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Washington County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus line 54 Services: Weatherization Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - EARLY HEAD START EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - HEAD START HOUSING/SHELTER - COMMUNITY CONNECT HOUSING/SHELTER - HOUSING REPAIR ASSISTANCE FOR BEAVERTON RESIDENTS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - HEALTHY FAMILIES OREGON INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - HOUSING FAMILY ADVOCACY PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES MATERIAL GOODS - SENIOR PORTABLE AC MONEY MANAGEMENT - FINANCIAL EDUCATION CLASSES If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. BEAVERTON MULTI-SERVICE CENTER
